## Highlights

Patch release on top of [0.5.0](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/tag/0.5.0). One user-facing fix to `createvm` networking:

- **`createvm` now works with bridge networks.** A static `--ip4` on a
    bridge libvirt network previously failed (`createvm` could only derive
    DNS/gateway from a NAT network's metadata). New flags let you supply them:
    - `--gateway <ip>` and `--dns <ip[,ip...]>` — required for a static
        `--ip4` on a bridge; **ignored for NAT** (still self-derived).
    - `--search-domain <dom[,dom...]>` — optional DNS search domain(s),
        honored on both NAT and bridge.
    - A static `--ip4` on a bridge without `--gateway`/`--dns` now fails fast
        with a message naming the missing flags.
- **`--ip4 <network>` (bare network name, no IP) now means DHCP on that
    network** — e.g. `createvm host web ubuntu2404 --ip4 vlan10`. A bare value
    is treated as a network name unless it's IP-ish (digits/dots, optional
    `/CIDR`), so a numeric typo still gets the clean "not a valid IPv4
    address" error rather than a confusing network lookup.

`--ip4 <ip>` (default network) and `--ip4 NETWORK,IP` are unchanged.

## Breaking changes

None.

## Install / upgrade

```bash
uv tool install --upgrade \
  https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/download/0.5.1/tkc_lvlab-0.5.1-py3-none-any.whl
```

## Supported hosts

Unchanged from 0.5.0 — Debian 12 (3.11), Debian 13 (3.13), AlmaLinux 10
(3.12), Fedora 44 (3.14). 0.5.0 was application-smoke validated on all four
(see [`docs-extra/host-validation.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.1/docs-extra/host-validation.md));
0.5.1 changes only `createvm`'s network-argument handling. The new bridge
path was confirmed end-to-end against a real bridge network.

## Also in this release (not user-facing)

- Test fix: the `lvlab`-path integration manifest now points at the `lvlab/`
    image cache so the suite runs clean on a host seeded via `lvlab init`
    (no bare-`/var/lib/libvirt/images/cloud-images/` precondition).
- New `scripts/hostcheck.sh` — a per-host release smoke (install the wheel +
    boot one guest), used for the 0.5.0 four-host validation.
